prompt: You are processing a batch of 20 data files, but several files have intentional corruption that you must detect, handle gracefully, and recover from. The data files are at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/data/batch/' named 'batch-001.csv' through 'batch-020.csv'. Each file has 50-200 rows of CSV data with headers: id, name, email, amount, date, status, category. Your task: 1) Read each file one at a time. Some files have the following issues: file may not exist (deleted or renamed), file may have malformed CSV (unescaped commas in fields, mismatched quotes), file may have missing headers or wrong column count, file may contain invalid data (non-numeric amounts, invalid dates, malformed email addresses), file may be empty (0 rows), file may be binary/corrupted (random bytes instead of CSV). 2) For each file you successfully read: validate every row against the expected schema, separate valid rows from invalid rows, log all validation errors with row number and field. 3) For files with errors: attempt recovery where possible (fix common CSV issues, trim whitespace, normalize date formats), skip unrecoverable files and log the reason. 4) After processing all 20 files: combine all valid rows from all files into a single sorted dataset at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/data/output/combined-valid.csv', write all invalid/rejected rows to '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/data/output/rejected-rows.csv' with an additional 'reason' column, write recovery-attempted rows to '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/data/output/recovered-rows.csv'. 5) Generate a batch processing report at '/tmp/benchmark-sandbox/reports/batch-processing-report.txt' with: per-file processing status (success/partial/failed), row counts (valid/invalid/recovered per file), total statistics across all files, detailed error catalog (every error type and how many times it occurred), and recommendations for fixing the source data. CRITICAL: This batch job processes $10M+ daily transactions. You must also: 1) Implement circuit breaker pattern. 2) Create dead letter queues for failed items. 3) Add idempotency checks to prevent duplicate processing. 4) Generate real-time dashboards with success/failure metrics. 5) Create auto-scaling triggers based on queue depth.
